mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS held near the zero lower bound since late 2008, the persistently low policy rate compresses equity discount rates and incentivizes a reach-for-yield rotation into stocks, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as rates remain near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.2 at 2010-06-15, unchanged near zero since Dec 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX forward returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly different from the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE remains above 9% while PAYEMS fails to post two consecutive months of net gains exceeding 100k, the labor market signals insufficient demand for durable consumption, leading equities to underperform over the following quarter as earnings growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9.0 AND PAYEMS 2-month net change < 100 thousand
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean triggered-return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in INDPRO and RSAFS confirm a synchronized production-consumption recovery cycle, which forward-looking equity markets price in as broadening earnings breadth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; the effect requires both series to co-move since either alone reflects only partial demand or supply signal.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=102.27 and RSAFS=362517 recovering in early 2010
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows forward 20d return distribution statistically indistinguishable from single-series trigger samples (ANOVA p > 0.10), indicating no genuine interaction effect beyond additive contributions.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST from post-crisis lows signals renewed household confidence and credit access, which historically leads broader risk asset appreciation over a quarterly horizon during the early recovery phase following a housing-led recession, but this relationship is regime-specific and does not hold in normal expansions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=672.0 thousand units, still near historic lows post-2008 housing crash
failure_condition: HOUST uptrend periods outside the immediate post-2008 recovery window (e.g., 2003-2006 boom or later normal cycles) show no significant positive correlation with forward SPX returns, or in-sample recovery-period Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect expanding real economic output and directly precede short-horizon equity gains as traders front-run improving corporate revenue trajectories within the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d SPX return stays below 0.02 across rolling 1-year windows, indicating no short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth exceeds 3%, it confirms a durable expansion phase that supports sustained corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as analysts revise growth forecasts upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=13248.2B as of t-5mo showing recovery growth
failure_condition: Instances with GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 3% show subsequent 60-day SPX returns not significantly different from baseline (p > 0.05), or negative in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL month-over-month change turns negative for two consecutive months, signaling incipient deflation risk, equity markets react negatively over the following month as deflation raises real debt burdens and threatens corporate margins despite near-zero policy rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change < 0 for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=217.579 as of t-2mo, inflation subdued post-crisis
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following two consecutive negative CPI prints are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Improving PAYEMS growth combined with persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS jointly signal that monetary accommodation is finally translating into labor market repair, a combination that historically triggers a short-horizon risk-on rally as markets price reduced tail risk of a double-dip recession; neither series alone carries the same signal strength.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=130570k as of t-1mo, FEDFUNDS=0.2 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: zero-rate policy accommodation with nascent labor recovery
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample forward 5d returns are statistically indistinguishable from PAYEMS-only trigger sample returns (p > 0.10), indicating no incremental interaction effect from low FEDFUNDS.
